data.title: Adobe in spotlight as Citi sees 'mixed' Q1 amid recent software weakness
data.description:
Adobe (NASDAQ:ADBE) was in the spotlight on Monday as Citi said it is “slightly cautious” on the Photoshop maker headed into its first-quarter results, amid recent weakness from some enterprise software companies.

Shares fell 1.3% in premarket trading.

“We are slightly cautious into Adobe’s FQ1 print given the softer outlook from CRM + other front-office names, though its valuation is less demanding vs. large-cap peers,” analyst Tyler Radke wrote in a note to clients. “Pricing may remain an important component in ADBE’s growth [algorithm] this year amid recent price updates to flagship apps and as weaker [Creative Cloud] web traffic could suggest paid user acquisition remains challenging.”

Radke kept his Neutral rating on the stock but lowered his price target to $490.

In recent months, Adobe upped the subscription price for 20GB of Photoshop by $5 to $14.99 a month, while it bumped Lightroom by $2 to $11.99 per month, effective January 15. Substance 3D will be raised by $10 per month to $59.99 per month, while the pre-paid plan goes to $599.98, effective March 25. However, Adobe also released a free Photoshop app for the iPhone last month.

When taken together, it suggests that Adobe can keep raising the prices on its core creative professional base in an effort to offset slower monetization down-market, “likely due to fiercer competition vs. Canva and other casual photo/video-editing freemium apps,” Radke suggested.

A consensus of analysts expect Adobe to earn $4.97 per share on $5.66B in revenue for the coming quarter.

data.title: NV5 announces $18 million in recent contract awards
data.description:
• NV5 Global (NASDAQ:NVEE) on Monday said it has been awarded $18 million in recent data center design and commissioning contracts throughout the U.S. and Asia.
  • NV5 will implement high-efficiency cooling and power distribution testing, inspection, certification and design solutions for both new and retrofitted facilities.
  • NVEE -0.71% premarket to $18.1.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Airbnb attracts a new bull rating from Jefferies
data.description:
Airbnb (NASDAQ:ABNB) pushed slightly higher in early trading on Monday after Jefferies upgraded the online travel stock to a Buy rating after having it set at Hold.

Analyst David Katz and his team believe lodging share gains will be augmented by an increased adoption of experiences. Airbnb (NASDAQ:ABNB) is seen as uniquely well positioned to capture that opportunity. In addition, Jefferies thinks Airbnb's (ABNB) growth will be further bolstered by take rate upside, driven mostly by the launch of sponsored listings.

"Recent results underscore ABNB's long runway for share gains in lodging, driven by demographic tailwinds, investments in underpenetrated markets, diversification into new marketing channels, and potential expansion into new listing types (e.g. hotels)."

Jefferies assigned a price target of $185 to Airbnb (ABNB). 

The consensus sell-side rating on Airbnb (ABNB) is Hold.

Shares of ABNB moved up 0.35% in premarket action to $134.86 vs. the 52-week trading range of $110.38 to $170.10.

data.title: Concentra acquires Physicians Health Center and OM Management
data.description:
• Concentra (NYSE:CON) on Monday announced the acquisition of the assets of Physicians Health Center (PHC) and OM Management (OMM).
  • The PHC Airport location at 6221 NW 36th Street, Virginia Gardens, Florida 33166 will become Concentra Miami Airport, while the PHC Hialeah location at 6990 NW 37th Avenue, Miami, Florida 33147 will become Concentra Hialeah East.
  • The OM Management corporate office location will become a satellite office for Concentra’s CMCA group.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Tinley's signs distribution agreements with Molson Coors distributors, changes name
data.description:
The Tinley Beverage Company (OTCQB:TNYBF) Monday said that it has expanded its sales and distribution network, executing exclusive distribution agreements in New Jersey and Connecticut.

Tinley's is also shipping out the first orders of beverages for its newest distribution partners.

Tinley's signed an exclusive distribution agreement with Hawthorne Heritage Beverage, a division of Kohler Distributing, a Molson Coors (NYSE:TAP) distributor and one of the largest in the state of New Jersey.

Furthermore, Tinley's (OTCQB:TNYBF) is changing its name from "The Tinley Beverage Company Inc." to "Beckett's Inc", to better align with the company's focus on developing and marketing the Beckett's brand.

data.title: KE Holdings announces inclusion of Class A ordinary shares in the Shanghai-Hong Kong Stock Connect and Shenzhen-Hong Kong Stock Connect programs
data.description:
• KE Holdings (NYSE:BEKE) on Monday said the company’s Class A ordinary shares traded on the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong have been included in the Shanghai-Hong Kong Stock Connect and the Shenzhen-Hong Kong Stock Connect programs, both effective on March 10.
  • Following the inclusion, eligible investors in Mainland China will have direct access to the trading of the company’s Class A ordinary shares.
  • BEKE -1.03% premarket to $24.01.
  • Source: Press Release
